headline: Harvest and feed now — 100°F closes Friday's window.
weather_notes: Do all outdoor work before 9 AM today — 94°F today is the last manageable morning before 100°F hits Friday through Saturday.
Skip hand watering today — zone 1 ran this morning and yesterday's 0.16" rain keeps soil adequately moist.
Apply soil feeds this morning — Friday's 100°F shrinks the usable outdoor window to pre-8 AM.
Hold the next zone run until after Saturday — let Saturday's 20% afternoon storm chance play out before scheduling the next irrigation cycle.

surfaced_tasks.title: Tip new blackberry primocanes if 3–4 ft tall
surfaced_tasks.reason: 13 days overdue; today's 94°F is the last workable morning window before Friday–Saturday 100°F closes outdoor access.

suggested_tasks.title: Harvest c2/c3/c4 beans this morning
suggested_tasks.description: At 56 days, Jade Bush (c3/c4) and Red Swan (c2) are past their harvest window — pick now before Friday's 100°F degrades remaining pods.

suggested_tasks.title: Feed e1–e4 tomatoes with fish emulsion
suggested_tasks.description: 33-day transplants have no logged fertilizer — apply this morning before Friday's 100°F narrows the early work window further.
